title: change `language_detection_threshold` default value
body: the current value of `language_detection_threshold` means that `language_detection_segments` will be 1 regardless of the user set value, the new value will ensure that `language_detection_segments` is respected

step_plan.summary: Update the default `language_detection_threshold` so that multi-segment language detection in `WhisperModel.transcribe()` does not stop after the first segment by default, making `language_detection_segments` effective without requiring user overrides.

steps.operation: Change the default value of `language_detection_threshold` in the `transcribe` signature and align the language-detection loop’s early-break behavior with the new default so multi-segment language detection can actually iterate past the first segment when confidence is low.
steps.reason: Today the default `language_detection_threshold=None` makes the language-detection loop break on the first segment, effectively ignoring `language_detection_segments` unless the user overrides the threshold. Setting a numeric default (and ensuring `None` still means “accept first segment immediately”, if that behavior is retained) makes `language_detection_segments` respected by default.
steps.side_effects: Behavior change for users who relied on the implicit `None` default: language detection may evaluate additional segments before deciding, which can slightly increase startup latency and may change detected language in ambiguous audio. Any downstream code/tests asserting single-segment language detection under defaults will need updating.
